notes:
No tweaks needed, but after a while of play, often the mouse stops working in menus like the tablet view, and also switching to big picture to try to exit the screen to other displays on the desktop.  The mouse is usually stuck on the display, and doesn't respond to any movement.  If you tab out of tablet view, I can move, look, and generally play the game fine, so mouse still technically works.  There is some bug between the mouse grab/lock on the window and the pointer overlay stopping working.  

Clicking frantically and button mashing the keyboard tends to kick it into working, but then you get this odd drunken lag to the mouse, almost it over-commits in one direction, but any movement to correct results in greatly over-exaggerated swaying that is enough to make you sick watching too close (which I tend to do).

Only way to resolve this is to quit the game and restart.  Always fun.

notes:
Game is generally playable at 20+ hours now, but noticed very high cpu utilization across all cores, which on my system is 20 cores, and 40 threads, all lit up to 80% during game play.  Ouch, and my tower becomes a furnace...

Found after some query among steam forum that windoze runs fairly low cpu, so this wasn't normal, and began enabling tweaks, where just disabling esync removed the crazy cpu usage across all cores to almost non-existent levels.  Winning!

I still can't get the game to run over 12fps in 4k, whether lowest or highest settings in graphics.  Moving resolution to 1080p gets up to 15fps, but still rather noticeable to the eye.  I get  60-100fps in most games even at 4k on this system, so something is still wrong here.

The mouse freeze issue still occurs, which is the most frustrating problem in use.

notes:
After an half an hour to an hour of play, the mouse will stop working on menus. This can be fixed in the short term by tabbing in and out each time you go into or out of a menu. In the long run, you have to restart the game.Some textures wouldn't load in without the launch option -force-opengl. Also, if the menu to activate console command vanishes every time you click, try using it with the game unpaused and your inventory open. Sometimes the game won't close properly, and even after killing the process, steam will refuse to play any games until I reboot.

tl;dr minor, manageable, but still annoying issues.

notes: Perfect on experimental branch. 60fps on max settings with steam controller.
title: Subnautica

notes:
It seems with Intel CPUs, the current stable build spikes the CPU easily. The current experimental build seems to fix this issue. AMD CPUs, going by the reports, do not have this problem with the current stable build.

Disabling ESYNC seemed to make it perform better. Forcing OpenGL will fix missing textures. If your game locks up when changing resolution, start a new game, then change resolution. Then it will at least save the resolution. Restart the game and you should be OK.

The actual launch options I use is: PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command% -force-opengl
title: Subnautica

title: Subnautica

notes:
As others already mentioned. There are mouse capturing issues with the game. I used Ubuntu 18.10 with the GNOME Desktop 3.30, in case you encounter the same.

Problem is, this mouse issue can occur anywhere between 4-30 minutes, but it renders the UI and/or swimming non-functional. Since that's about the entire game, it makes it highly unstable to play. The issue can be remedies by doing the following:

1. Add "-popupwindow" to the launch options.
2.  Set the game to your desktop resolution, but not fullscreen.

This runs the game in a borderless window mode, fixing the capturing issue.
title: Subnautica

notes: As mentioned by others, the mouse capturing can be fiddly. A last resort, is to run the game windowed: Got to the options menu and disable fullscreen mode. Then, use the following paremeters to launch the game:  " -screen-width 1920 -screen-height 1080", adjusted to your desired size of cause.

notes:
The mouse.. problem. While using the builder after some minutes the mouse pointer got stuck and wont move again. So its impossible to select different items to build.
A quick and dirty solution was: Tabbing out of the game and tabbing back in again. Now the mouse can be used to select diffent types in the builder menu. Next problem: now the world is spinning like crazy. Easy solution: Tabbing out and back in again solves the spinning so you can build stuff. But again.. mouse will be stuck again in builder menu. Easy solution.. tab out and back in again.. Its annoying but playable ;) Runs great (except those mouse issue).

notes: Experimental branch fixes the mouse issue. I used the launch options "-window-width 1920 -window-height 1080 -popupwindow". I played with everything at max except Screen Space Reflections (kept at low), motion blur off and vsync off. I got about 70fps in my 30 minutes of playing on the experimental branch. Great to see this working well now!!
title: Subnautica

notes: Use the experimental branch to fix the mouse problem. Then the game will run perfect. I ran the game with everything at max (including motion blur) and vsync off and got a consistent 50fps, with drops to 40fps when loading new areas. Turning some settings down (mainly screen-space reflections to "Off" and water quality to "Medium") gets the game to run at 60fps+ for me, but honestly if I didn't have the Steam FPS counter then I wouldn't have noticed that the game wasn't running at 60fps with everything at max. Great experience, now I actually want to play the game!
title: Subnautica

notes: After switching to the experimental branch of the game and also adding the launch option -force-opengl, the game runs mostly without issue. I had to disable surface reflections in the game's options because they were causing severe graphical glitching. Presumably because of forcing the use of opengl, I am not able to change the level of water detail, which is unfortunate. When inside a certain place in the game I experienced a pretty drastic hit to performance. However, even with these hickups I've mentioned here, on the whole I found the game to be playable and enjoyable.
title: Subnautica

notes: It was working perfectly before but I had to reinstall my computer and it stopped working. I would push the "Play" button and it would show "Running..." beside the name of the game but nothing would happen. I had to close Steam to force it to kill the game or kill the processes myself (including Steam processes, not just the games. Otherwise any changes to the launch options wouldn't start the game, even the right ones). The only launch options that made it start is : PROTON_USE_WINED3D11=1 %command%  . Now everything seems okay.
title: Subnautica

notes: No matter what version of Proton I used I kept on having Subnautica hang before showing a Window.  FYI "sudo killall Subnautica.exe" was needed to kill the zombie Subnautica process otherwise a reboot was required.  I'm not sure if there is something wrong with my vulkan setup but PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1 PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 was required to get Subnautica to startup
